task:
[Read from: /home/spencer/Repositories/predictability-aware-training/plan.md, /home/spencer/Repositories/predictability-aware-training/progress.md]

RED-TEAM the code in /home/spencer/Repositories/predictability-aware-training (model.py, train.py). This is a research harness training a GPT-style MoE with a jointly-trained expert-activation predictor; the scientific claim rests on this code being correct. Adversarially hunt for bugs that could invalidate results, specifically: (1) the MoE dispatch math (per-expert gather/scatter, top-k weight renormalization, the `w.sum(dim=-1)` weighting); (2) the Switch-style load-balancing loss formulation; (3) the predictability loss — target construction (soft scatter of realized top-k), whether gradients flow to the backbone as intended in joint mode, and whether posthoc mode truly freezes everything else; (4) hit@k metric computation (does it measure top-k set overlap correctly?); (5) train/eval contamination — the FineWeb streaming generator is shared between training steps and evaluate() calls; is eval actually held out?; (6) LR schedule, autocast bf16 correctness, checkpoint/load issues (posthoc loads a full state dict then re-initializes predictor? check ParameterDict init and load ordering); (7) anything about horizons indexing (layer_hiddens[l] predicts topks[l+h]). READ-ONLY: do not modify files, do not run training (GPU is busy with a run). You may run quick CPU python snippets with the .venv-rocm or .venv interpreters to verify suspicions. Report each finding with severity (critical/major/minor), file:line, and a concrete fix.

criteria.must: Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable
Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable

criteriaSatisfied.evidence: 8 concrete findings with file:line and severity (no blockers/criticals): model.py:76-78 (top-1 bal fraction, minor), train.py:36-46 (eval stream caveats, minor), train.py:77+model.py baseline predictor weight-decay drift (minor), train.py:80-81 (LR resurrection, minor), model.py:130-140 (gradient-shaping caveat, minor), train.py:69-70 (posthoc ckpt leakage hazard, minor), model.py:77 (bf16 argmax tie-break, minor), train.py:46 (horizon>=n_layers crash, minor). Core math verified correct by numerical equivalence tests.

commandsRun.summary: dispatch max err 1.1e-18; bal matches documented top-1 formula but is 2x off top-2 formulation; hit@k err 0.0 vs set-overlap reference
joint: backbone grads flow from loss_pred; posthoc: only predictor params trainable, all receive grad; baseline lambda=0: AdamW weight decay shrinks predictor heads (0.2364->0.2350 / 200 steps)

childReport.diffSummary: No diff — read-only red-team review; no files modified
reviewFindings: minor: model.py:76-78 - Switch bal loss uses top-1 argmax fraction while model dispatches top-2; under-counts second-choice load (1.04 vs 2.14 measured)
minor: train.py:33 - no EOS separator between FineWeb documents; tiktoken can raise on literal special tokens in web text
minor: train.py:77 - baseline (lambda_pred=0) still weight-decays predictor heads via AdamW; posthoc control starts from shrunken-random init
minor: train.py:80-81 - cosine LR resurrects for s in (steps, 2*steps); warmup never completes if steps<100
minor: train.py:69-70 - posthoc loads full state dict incl. predictor; a joint-trained ckpt silently leaks a pre-trained predictor into the 'posthoc control'
minor: model.py:77 - top1 argmax computed on bf16 logits while dispatch uses fp32 probs; near-tie mismatch (use probs.argmax)
minor: train.py:46 - --horizons >= n_layers yields empty head list and ZeroDivisionError at evaluate()

note: model.py:130-140 - predictability loss shapes backbone only via pre-MoE hidden h (verified router grad None); routing itself is not pulled toward predictions - mechanism claim should be stated precisely
childReport.manualNotes: plan.md/progress.md referenced in the task do not exist (PLAN.md/README.md/RESULTS.md do). Core scientific machinery - MoE dispatch, top-k renormalization, w.sum weighting, hit@k set overlap, soft-target predictor CE, posthoc freeze, horizons indexing, train/eval disjointness - is verified correct by numerical equivalence and gradient probes. The headline results in README.md are not invalidated by anything found; the highest-value follow-ups are the posthoc ckpt-leakage guard (finding 6 in review) and the baseline predictor weight-decay drift (finding 3).
